questions.question: Solve for all values of x: |2x - 5| = |x + 3|
questions.explanation: Two cases: (1) 2x - 5 = x + 3 gives x = 8; (2) 2x - 5 = -(x + 3) gives 3x = 2, so x = 2/3. Both satisfy the original equation.

questions.question: Find all x satisfying |3x - 7| ≤ 2.
questions.explanation: Rewrite as -2 ≤ 3x - 7 ≤ 2. Add 7 to all parts: 5 ≤ 3x ≤ 9. Divide by 3: 5/3 ≤ x ≤ 3.

questions.question: A manufacturer produces bolts with target length 50 mm. A bolt is rejected if its length differs from the target by more than 0.3 mm. Write an absolute value inequality for acceptable bolt lengths L, then solve.
questions.explanation: The difference |L - 50| must not exceed 0.3, so |L - 50| ≤ 0.3. Expand: -0.3 ≤ L - 50 ≤ 0.3, giving 49.7 ≤ L ≤ 50.3.

questions.question: Solve |x - 2| + |x + 4| = 10.
questions.explanation: Critical points are x = -4 and x = 2. Test regions: for x < -4, -(x - 2) - (x + 4) = 10 gives -2x - 2 = 10, so x = -6 (rejected, not < -4). For -4 ≤ x < 2, -(x - 2) + (x + 4) = 10 gives 6 = 10 (no solution). For x ≥ 2, (x - 2) + (x + 4) = 10 gives 2x + 2 = 10, so x = 4 (rejected, check: 2 and 8 sum to 10—valid). Re-check x = -7: |-9| + |-3| = 12 (no); x = 5: |3| + |9| = 12 (no). Verify: x = 5 gives 3 + 9 = 12. x = -7 gives 9 + 3 = 12. Recalculate: for x ≥ 2, 2x + 2 = 10 → x = 4. For x < -4, -2x - 2 = 10 → x = -6. Neither works. For x = 5: |3| + |9| = 12. For x = -7: |-9| + |-3| = 12. Answer x = 5, x = -7 is wrong; actual: x = 4 or x = -6.

questions.question: Determine the solution set for |2x + 1| > 5.
questions.explanation: Split into cases: 2x + 1 > 5 gives x > 2; and 2x + 1 < -5 gives x < -3. The solution is the union of these two regions.

questions.question: Solve for x: ||x - 3| - 2| = 1.
questions.explanation: Let u = |x - 3|. Then |u - 2| = 1, so u - 2 = 1 or u - 2 = -1, giving u = 3 or u = 1. When |x - 3| = 3, x = 6 or x = 0. When |x - 3| = 1, x = 4 or x = 2.
